title: Heatwave Alert: Cuttack orders morning classes from 6:30 AM - 9 AM - Odisha TV
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
In view of the prevailing heatwave conditions, the Cuttack district administration on Sunday revised the functioning hours of schools and Anganwadi centres to ensure the safety and well-being of children. The Office of the District Magistrate and Collector, Cuttack, issued an official order on April 19, directing all educational institutions in the district to operate only during early morning hours.
As per the order, all government, government-aided, and private schools in Cuttack will conduct classes from 6:30 AM and close by 9:00 AM until further notice. Similarly, all Anganwadi centres across the district have also been instructed to function only in the morning hours and shut operations by 9:00 AM.
The decision has been taken as a precautionary measure to protect children from the intense heatwave sweeping across the region.
It has also emphasized the need for adequate precautionary measures within school premises, including the availability of safe drinking water and minimizing outdoor activities during operational hours.
The order will remain in force until new instructions are issued. The district administration has warned that any violation of the directive will be taken seriously, and necessary action will be initiated as per rules.

title: Mercury to Touch 45°C in Odisha, Heatwave to Persist Till April 17 - pragativadi.com
full_text:
Odisha braces for an intense heatwave as temperatures are expected to soar to 45°C over the next five days, according to the Bhubaneswar Meteorological Centre.
Regional Meteorological Centre Director Manorama Mohanty said coastal and interior parts of the state will continue to experience extreme heat and humidity until April 17. She warned that relief from the scorching conditions is unlikely in the coming days.
The heatwave has already gripped several districts, with daytime temperatures rising steadily. Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, and western Odisha towns such as Sambalpur and Jharsuguda recorded severe heat conditions. Meteorologists said the dry northwesterly winds and clear skies are intensifying the heat across the region.
Health officials advised residents to avoid outdoor activities during peak hours and stay hydrated. Authorities also urged people to take precautions against heatstroke and dehydration.
The weather department said the mercury will remain high across Odisha, with no significant rainfall expected until mid-April. The prolonged heatwave has disrupted daily life, affecting outdoor workers and commuters.

title: Heatwave Alert: Schools, Anganwadis Shift To Morning Classes In Cuttack - Ommcom News
search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
full_text:
Cuttack: In view of prevailing heatwave-like conditions across the district, the Cuttack District Administration on Sunday revised the timings of all educational institutions and Anganwadi centres, directing them to function during early morning hours to ensure the safety of children.
According to an official order issued by the Office of the District Magistrate and Collector, Cuttack (District Emergency Operation Centre), all government, government-aided, and private schools in the district will now conduct classes from 6:30 am and conclude by 9:00 am. The revised schedule has been introduced as a precautionary measure in response to rising daytime temperatures.
Similarly, all Anganwadi centres under the district have been instructed to operate within the same time frame and close by 9:00 am. The move aims to minimise exposure of children to extreme heat during peak hours of the day.
The order further directed all heads of institutions and concerned authorities to ensure strict compliance with the revised schedule. Educational institutions have also been advised to make adequate arrangements, including the provision of safe drinking water and strict avoidance of outdoor activities during operational hours.
The administration has warned that any violation of the directive will be viewed seriously and may attract action under relevant provisions.
Officials said that weather conditions will be monitored on a daily basis, and further instructions will be issued if required. The current order will remain in force until it is revised or withdrawn by the authorities.
The decision comes amid a sharp rise in temperatures across Odisha, where several districts have been experiencing intense heatwave conditions over the past few days.
